channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-23
item.description:
Our community has seen exciting progress across multiple projects recently. In **scienceverse/metacheck**, Lakens addressed key issues with code checks and paper lists, while also adding new functions to load paper databases from **scienceverse/papers**. Meanwhile, **giladfeldman/docpluck** saw significant updates, including documentation improvements and fixes for table rendering issues. Additionally, **shaheedazaad/auto-zcurve** and **giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward** both received important updates, with a revised validation note and added citation in the former, and a new Chrome Webstore link in the latter. These connections between projects demonstrate the collaborative spirit of our community, and we celebrate the progress being made to improve our research tools.

91 commits across 6 active repositories this week.

Dormant repos revived: output-it-forward

Most active:

coarse30 commits
regcheck30 commits
docpluck19 commits
metacheck8 commits
escicheck3 commits

item.title: regcheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

Work was done on the "staging" branch, where a commit fixed OSF and mutlistudy bugs, as stated in the commit message "fix OSF + mutlistudy bugs". Another commit on the "staging" branch hardened security features, according to the message "harden security features". No other branches were worked on in these commits. The fixes and security hardening were the only changes made in these commits. The "staging" branch is the only branch that has been modified since the last update. These two commits comprise all the work done since the last update.

1 commit(s) today:

  • 5b53ad6 [staging] fix OSF + mutlistudy bugs — Jamie Cummins
  • 71be3be [staging] harden security features — Jamie Cummins
  • fbd5030 [staging] firm up qwen formatting — Jamie Cummins
  • f775856 [staging] improve qwen — Jamie Cummins
  • 724d020 [staging] re-add qwen! — Jamie Cummins
  • 89a1470 [staging] push for redeploy — Jamie Cummins
  • baebc81 [staging] hunting down osf bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 2f5eaaa [staging] improve logging — Jamie Cummins
  • 7fb80fe [staging] improve error message — Jamie Cummins
  • a2f7302 [staging] fix osf bug — Jamie Cummins
  • d2089d5 [staging] remove groq endpoints — Jamie Cummins
  • 51b9ba6 [staging] harden json handling — Jamie Cummins
  • 6cdde08 [staging] fix oss bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 1fd2de7 [staging] use more efficient storage — Jamie Cummins
  • 4b7a527 [staging] patch oss SDK handler — Jamie Cummins
  • e514303 [staging] fix oss handling bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 599489b [staging] harden qwen handling — Jamie Cummins
  • 8e7e452 [staging] improve embedding efficiency, purge dead code — Jamie Cummins
  • 88e039c [staging] replace deprecated llama — Jamie Cummins
  • 80dcf34 [staging] harden osf handling again — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →

item.title: output-it-forward: activity update
item.description:
giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward

The `main` branch was updated with a commit that added a Chrome Webstore link for OIF, as stated in the commit message "Add Chrome Webstore link for OIF". No other branches were worked on in this set of commits. This specific change adds a link to the Chrome Webstore, presumably to make it easier for users to access or install the OIF extension. The commit does not mention any other changes or additions beyond the Chrome Webstore link. There are no other branches or commits to report on in this update.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
